tools.name: memory__search
tools.title: Search memory
tools.description: Find memories by literal text, ranked by match count. Matches the query as ONE case-insensitive substring (not semantic, not tokenized) across titles/bodies/tags - so search a SINGLE keyword, not a phrase. Returns path+snippet+score (score = number of matches, not a relevance %), never full bodies. Use when you have a keyword to rank by; to browse the folder tree instead, use memory__list. Zero hits? try a synonym.

query.description: Literal text to find (case-insensitive). Matched as ONE exact substring across titles/bodies/tags - not semantic, not split into words - so a multi-word query matches only that exact phrase; search a SINGLE distinctive keyword. e.g. pkce

folder.description: Folder, POSIX, no leading slash (trailing slash optional), e.g. work/tasks. Matches that folder only - not a string prefix. Omit = whole memory

tags.description: Tags to filter by (frontmatter tags: or inline #tag), AND-matched (all must be present), case-sensitive, bare without #. e.g. [project, active]. Omit = no tag filter

limit.description: Optional. Max results in this page; defaults to 20, capped at 50.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination token - pass the previous response's nextCursor here verbatim for the next page. Omit for the first page; never construct or edit it.

tools.name: memory__read
tools.title: Read memory
tools.description: Read ONE memory by its exact path: returns full frontmatter + body. Use a path you got from memory__search/list, a prior write/edit, or the user - do not invent or guess one from a title. No known path? use memory__search first.

path.description: Exact POSIX .md address - not a title or search query. Case-sensitive, no leading slash. e.g. work/tasks/foo.md

tools.name: memory__list
tools.title: Browse memory
tools.description: Browse the memory as a folder tree: the files and subfolders under a folder, 2 levels deep by default, with per-folder file counts - no bodies, no ranking. Use to see what exists and how it is organized, or to list every note carrying a tag (tags filter); have a keyword to rank by instead? use memory__search. A folder shown without its own entries has more inside: call memory__list again with that folder. Folders over the per-folder entry limit are flagged truncated and not expanded - browse them directly or narrow with memory__search.

folder.description: Folder, POSIX, no leading slash (trailing slash optional), e.g. work/tasks. Matches that folder only - not a string prefix. Omit = whole memory

depth.description: Optional. 1 = direct children of the folder only; 2 (default) = also expand each subfolder one more level.

tags.description: Tags to filter by (frontmatter tags: or inline #tag), AND-matched (all must be present), case-sensitive, bare without #. e.g. [project, active]. Omit = no tag filter

folder.description: The browsed folder, normalized; empty = memory root

files.description: Recursive file count inside this folder

truncated.description: True = this folder was NOT expanded (over the per-folder entry limit or the response budget); call memory__list with this folder path to see inside

files.description: Recursive file count inside this folder

truncated.description: True = this folder was NOT expanded (over the per-folder entry limit or the response budget); call memory__list with this folder path to see inside

entries.description: Direct children, present when this folder is expanded (within depth and limits)

truncated.description: True = something was omitted anywhere in this response (a folder over the per-folder limit, or the response budget); narrow with a subfolder or memory__search

files.description: Total files under the browsed folder (after the tags filter)

tools.name: memory__write
tools.title: Write memory
tools.description: Create a NEW memory or fully OVERWRITE an existing one at path - replaces the entire body and frontmatter (idempotent; the saved memory persists for every AI). Use when you have the complete final content. To change only part of an existing memory, use memory__edit. Store durable knowledge and notes only - never secrets, passwords, API keys, one-time codes, or payment/government identifiers (these are refused).

path.description: Exact POSIX .md address - not a title or search query. Case-sensitive, no leading slash. e.g. work/tasks/foo.md

body.description: The complete Markdown body, excluding frontmatter (no --- fences). write overwrites the entire existing body.

frontmatter.description: YAML metadata as a key->value map (no --- fences), e.g. {"type":"task","status":"active","tags":["project"]}. write replaces the whole map; edit shallow-merges top-level keys (nested values replaced wholesale; a key cannot be removed via edit - use write to fully replace)

tools.name: memory__edit
tools.title: Edit memory
tools.description: Amend an EXISTING memory in place: mode=str_replace swaps one exact text match (old_str -> new_str) without resending the note - the default choice for small changes; append adds to the end; replace overwrites the whole body; frontmatter shallow-merges. Fails with not-found if the path does not exist - use memory__write to create. Do not introduce secrets, passwords, API keys, one-time codes, or payment/government identifiers (these are refused).

path.description: Exact POSIX .md address - not a title or search query. Case-sensitive, no leading slash. e.g. work/tasks/foo.md

body.description: Markdown body, excluding frontmatter (no --- fences). With mode=replace it becomes the new body; with mode=append it is added to the end. Not used with mode=str_replace. Omit to change only frontmatter.

frontmatter.description: YAML metadata as a key->value map (no --- fences), e.g. {"type":"task","status":"active","tags":["project"]}. write replaces the whole map; edit shallow-merges top-level keys (nested values replaced wholesale; a key cannot be removed via edit - use write to fully replace)

mode.description: Optional. "str_replace" swaps old_str for new_str in place; "append" adds body to the end; omit or "replace" overwrites the whole body. Frontmatter always shallow-merges regardless.

old_str.description: mode=str_replace only. The exact existing body text to replace - must match verbatim (including whitespace) and appear exactly once. Read the memory first to copy it.

new_str.description: mode=str_replace only. The replacement text. Omit to delete old_str.

tools.name: memory__delete
tools.title: Delete memory
tools.description: Soft-delete (forget) a memory by path; recoverable from git history. Returns not-found if the path does not exist. To remove only part of a memory, use memory__edit.

path.description: Exact POSIX .md address - not a title or search query. Case-sensitive, no leading slash. e.g. work/tasks/foo.md
